risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Patients were randomly assigned according to the medical record numbers to receive a preoperative dose of levosimendan ... in group L or a placebo ... in group C." Comment: Allocation based on medical record numbers is an explicit predictable/non-random rule, so the sequence generation was not truly random despite the study being described as randomized.

Quote: "Patients were randomly assigned according to the medical record numbers to receive a preoperative dose of levosimendan ... in group L or a placebo ... in group C." Comment: Allocation based on medical record numbers is a predictable rule specifically indicating that the upcoming assignment could be foreseen before enrolment; this does not provide concealment and is a high-risk method for allocation concealment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The patients with single vessel disease requiring graft to left anterior descending artery, double vessel disease, and triple vessel disease with LVEF less than 30% and myocardial perfusion scan showing viability of the affected territory were included in the randomized, double blind study." and "Patients were randomly assigned ... to receive a preoperative dose of levosimendan ... or a placebo ... dissolved in NS to mimic the color of levosimendan in group C. The method of administration was similar in both groups." Comment: The trial explicitly states it was double blind and describes a matched placebo made to mimic the active drug, with similar administration in both groups. This supports blinding of participants and treating personnel and suggests blinding was likely maintained. Outcomes were largely objective perioperative/hemodynamic and clinical events, further reducing concern that any residual knowledge of allocation would materially affect outcomes.

Quote: "The patients with single vessel disease requiring graft to left anterior descending artery, double vessel disease, and triple vessel disease with LVEF less than 30% and myocardial perfusion scan showing viability of the affected territory were included in the randomized, double blind study." Quote: "Patients were randomly assigned ... to receive a preoperative dose of levosimendan ... or a placebo ... dissolved in NS to mimic the color of levosimendan in group C. The method of administration was similar in both groups." Comment: Outcome assessment appears to have been conducted mainly via objective/standardized measures and clinical events: hemodynamic monitoring (CI, PCWP, HR, ABP), mortality, need for IABP/CPB, renal dysfunction by serum creatinine, and arrhythmias. The trial is explicitly described as double blind and used matched placebo administration, with no subjective patient-reported outcomes identified. Even if some event ascertainment involved clinicians, the reported outcomes are largely objective and unlikely to be materially influenced by knowledge of allocation.

Quote: "Fifty patients with low LV function (<30%) admitted to U. N. Mehta Institute of Cardiology and Research Center was enrolled for the study during July-December 2012. The patients were randomly divided into two groups of 25 each... The data from these 50 patients were included in statistical analysis." Comment: The paper reports 25 randomized per arm and explicitly states that data from all 50 patients were included in the statistical analysis, indicating no post-randomization loss on the analysed outcomes. Deaths are reported as outcomes ("Mortality observed in one patient in group L and three patients in group in the time span of 5-30 days after surgery"), not unexplained attrition. This is consistent with essentially complete follow-up and no arm imbalance in missing outcome data.
